publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-01-12 23:01:33 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Papers concerning Willcox's career as professor of economics and statistics at Cornell, including extensive material on American social and political issues, as well as Willcox's personal and family papers.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Professor of economics and statistics at Cornell University.

relatedmaterial.head: RELATED MATERIALS
relatedmaterial.p: Includes collections #14-10-821, #14-10-mf32, #14-10-tr24, tr25, tr23, and tr22, and #14-10-m.76, m.376, and m.466.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Walter Francis Willcox papers, #14-10-504. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, tributes, printed and draft articles and notes, and genealogies and other family papers. Subjects include Willcox's involvement in the United States census of 1900, the International Statistical Institute, Amherst College alumni activity, and statistical analysis of various social problems, including the registration of births and deaths and voter registration, Afro-American crime statistics, race relations, and Prohibition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: The collection also includes tape recordings celebrating Willcox's 90th, 95th, and 100th birthdays; family correspondence, including letters to and from Alice Eloise Work (Mrs. Walter Willcox), and his son Bertram F. who was in France with the American Ambulance Field Service.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Also, material relating to his work with the William Allen White Committee to Defend America by Aiding the Allies, comment on American political practices and statecraft, addresses and articles pertaining to Cornell University, and work concerning Congressional reapportionment. Tape recordings pertain to Andrew Dickson White, Jacob Gould Schurman, and Charles Kendall Adams.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Collection of anecdotes concerning various members of the Cornell University faculty and administrators, collected by Willcox for use by the Cornell Alumni News.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: 90th Birthday Dinner address, "The Disunited Nations: A Novel Experiment in the Balance of Power," and cover letter, April 2, 1951.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Memo by Loren Petry on the origin of the Willcox-Becker Luncheon Club (sometimes called the Thursday Luncheon Club), and comments by Walter Willcox on the club, plus his memories of his years (1899-1901) as chief statistician of the 12 U.S. Census. There is also an interesting reference to Edmund Ezra Day and freedom of speech.

did.unittitle: St-W correspondence re statistics and reapportionment for U.S. Congress

Walter F. Willcox. "A Notable College Class" (Amherst, 1884 )
